title: crambe maritima subsp. pinnatifida
intro: Crambe maritima subsp. pinnatifida, also known as sea kale, is a plant species that belongs to the Brassicaceae family. It is a perennial herb with edible leaves and stems that have been used for centuries in traditional medicine. The medicinal properties of Crambe maritima subsp. pinnatifida include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which have been attributed to its high content of glucosinolates and other bioactive compounds. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including arthritis, digestive issues, and skin problems, and can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or raw in salads.
